title: Alleviating Spaceport Traffic by Rewarding Operators Act of 2025
short_title: Alleviating Spaceport Traffic by Rewarding Operators Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill lets the Department of Transportation create a special program to give money to companies that run rocket launch sites. The companies can use this money to build or fix roads, parking lots, and other transportation things that help people get to and from the rocket launch areas safely. The companies get more money if they had more rocket launches the year before, and they can get extra money if other groups help pay for the projects too. The program will last until the year 2030 and is meant to help make it easier for people to travel to see rocket launches.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Alleviating Spaceport Traffic by Rewarding Operators Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Intermodal Transportation Infrastructure Improvement Pilot Program
sections.text:
The Secretary of Transportation can create a test program to give money grants to companies that run rocket launch and landing sites. The money helps these companies build, fix, and maintain roads, parking areas, and other transportation facilities at their sites.

To get a grant, a company must apply to the Secretary using the required forms and provide all needed information. The company must prove their project follows the rules about how grant money can be used. They must also agree to keep records about the grant and share those records with the Secretary or government auditors when asked.

Companies can only use grant money for projects that build, fix, maintain, or improve infrastructure and facilities that are at or next to a launch or landing site. These projects must directly help with transportation safety or moving people and cargo. The improved facilities must be made available to others under fair business terms.

Starting after fiscal year two thousand twenty six, the Secretary can give qualifying companies a yearly grant. The amount depends on how many rocket launches happened the previous year. For each launch done under a full license, the company gets two hundred fifty thousand dollars. For each launch done under a permit, the company gets one hundred thousand dollars. However, no company can get more than two million five hundred thousand dollars per year, except for special bonus grants.

If a launch site is next to a federal launch range, the Secretary decides which company gets the grant if more than one applies. If one company operates multiple launch sites next to the same federal range, those sites count as just one site for grant purposes.

Companies can get bonus grants if other organizations help pay for their projects. If a state, local government, tribal government, or private company gives the operator money equal to their federal grant, the Secretary can give them a bonus grant worth twenty five percent of their original grant the next year. If these other organizations give twice as much money as the federal grant, the bonus can be fifty percent. The matching money must be given in the same year as the federal grant. A company cannot get both types of bonus grants in the same year. These bonus grants do not count toward the yearly limit.

All grant money comes from existing transportation funds. The total amount of all grants cannot exceed twenty million dollars per year. If there is not enough money, the Secretary will first reduce or eliminate bonus grants, then reduce regular grants proportionally.

Covered transportation activity means moving people or property to, from, or within a launch site using vehicles, boats, trains, aircraft, or pipelines. Launch, launch site, launch vehicle, reentry site, and reentry vehicle have the same meanings as defined in federal space law. An operator is a person licensed by the Secretary to run a launch or landing site. A qualified entity is any state, local, territorial, or tribal government or private company, or any combination of these. Secretary means the Secretary of Transportation.

This pilot program ends on October first, two thousand thirty.
